(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
July 30,2004
Michele L. Robinson
8738 Bronson Drive
Granite Bay, CA 95746
Re
Your Request for fnformal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-04-151
Dear Ms. Robinson
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding whether the Political
Reform Act (the "Act')' would restrict you from participating in certain activities outside
your employrnent as a public official. Since you do_not ask a question governed by the
Act, we are only able to provide general assistance.2
The Act prohibits apublic official from making or participating in the making of a
governmental decision in which he or she has a financial interest. (Section 87100.)
Since your question does not address a govemmental decision, we are unable to analyze
your facts further. However, we suggest you consult your agency's statement of
incompatible activities, if applicable, and contact the Attorney General's office at (916)
324-5765 to discuss whether Govemment code section 1090 may apply. we have
included a document entitled, "Can I Vote?" and an advice letter from our office to
provide you with general information about the Act and outside income.
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
' Sir"" you have not posed a question relating to the Act, w-e are treating your request as one for
informal assistance pursuant to regulation I 8329(c)(copy enclosed).2


File No. I-04-l5l
Page No. 2
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
r.
By:
S
PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Political Reform Act restricts a public official from participating in certain activities outside their employment.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide specific analysis because the requester's question does not address a governmental decision as defined by the Act. The FPPC recommends consulting the agency's statement of incompatible activities and the Attorney General's office regarding Government Code section 1090.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; this is an informal assistance response (I-04-151) indicating the request was outside FPPC's purview. Letter provides general guidance and referrals instead of specific advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Political Reform Act restricts a public official from participating in certain activities outside their employment.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide specific analysis because the requester's question does not address a governmental decision as defined by the Act. The FPPC recommends consulting the agency's statement of incompatible activities and the Attorney General's office regarding Government Code section 1090.
